decision_context: Choose 2-3 features from CONQUEST/HELEN OS roadmap for next 6-8 weeks

candidates.name: Delayed Decay Mechanic
candidates.description: Deterministic entropy system: world degrades when user absent/present, maintenance required
candidates.rationale: Preserves v0.0 alive-world constraint; minimal scope; highest certainty

candidates.name: Federation (Multi-Town Proof Exchange)
candidates.description: Multiple sealed towns exchange cross-receipts; test non-interference under federation
candidates.rationale: Validates sovereign enclave architecture; high value; moderate risk; proves Path A

candidates.name: Faction Diplomacy Suite
candidates.description: Negotiation, treaty, betrayal mechanics; uses federation for proof exchange
candidates.rationale: Makes federation tangible in game; enables emergent play; moderate scope; requires federation

candidates.name: Supply Chain & Scarcity
candidates.description: Resource tracking, trade routes, scarcity mechanics, storage limits
candidates.rationale: Large scope; high design complexity; powerful but risky; can defer to v0.2

candidates.name: SCF Integration (Coherence Filtering)
candidates.description: Feed SCF diagnostics into HELEN's decision evidence; measure decision quality improvement
candidates.rationale: Smallest scope; highest certainty; enables federation; low risk; unlocks HELEN autonomy

decision_options.name: Foundation Path (Low Risk)
decision_options.rationale: Start with certainty. Decay proves world model; SCF unlocks HELEN. Foundation for federation.

decision_options.name: Breakthrough Path (Medium Risk)
decision_options.rationale: Validate sovereign enclave architecture directly. Skip decay, prove proof system.

decision_options.name: Complete Path (High Risk)
decision_options.rationale: Do everything. Requires hard deadline push. High value if successful; risky.

decision_options.name: Safe Scaling Path
decision_options.rationale: Build out CONQUEST world fully. Defer federation proof. Strong gameplay, lower breakthrough value.

trade_offs.description: Federation proves architecture (Path A validation). Decay proves world consistency.
trade_offs.recommendation: Both eventually; federation first for institutional credibility

trade_offs.description: Diplomacy is game experience. Supply chain is game depth.
trade_offs.recommendation: Diplomacy depends on federation; supply chain is independent. Diplomacy first.

trade_offs.description: Federation is proof. Supply chain is complexity.
trade_offs.recommendation: Federation validates breakthrough; supply chain can wait. Federation first.

questions_for_helen: Which option best validates Path A (operational proof of constitutional AI)?
What is the dependency order if we want to prove federation by week 8?
What is the minimum viable feature set for institutional credibility?
Should we defer supply chain entirely to v0.2 or split it?
